hero.title: Solar plant data intelligence, from SCADA alarms to cleaning action
hero.subtitle: SCADA shows kWh after the loss. Taypro's stack adds labelled cleaning telemetry (NECTYR, live) and ORION plant intelligence (roadmap), correlating soiling, weather, and yield for utility asset owners.

cards.heading: Physical robots, operations data, plant intelligence
cards.subheading: Data intelligence starts with execution proof, then moves to predictive O&M.

card0.title: Robots execute cleaning
card0.body: GLYDE, NYUMA, HELYX fleets generate block-level pass logs every cycle, the raw events SCADA does not capture.

card1.title: NECTYR: live operations data
card1.body: Scheduling, coverage maps, AMC tickets, and monthly OPEX settlement. Live since 2022 on 150+ utility sites.

card2.title: ORION: plant intelligence roadmap
card2.body: Weather-normalised PR drift, dust-storm triggers, yield forecasting. Early access for select IPPs under NDA.

card3.title: SCADA complement, not replacement
card3.body: Inverter and meter data stay in your SCADA. Taypro adds the cleaning and soiling-action layer.

card4.title: Labelled telemetry moat
card4.body: 11 billion+ annual panel passes feed models, cleaning events tied to array blocks, not generic weather feeds alone.

card5.title: Attach at AMC renewal
card5.body: ORION designed to upgrade existing Taypro fleets on rails already operating, not a cold-start software sale.

pillars.heading: What plant data intelligence should deliver

pillar0.title: Block-level cleaning proof
pillar0.body: Which panels were cleaned last night, evidence lenders and O&M governance require at 100 MW scale.

pillar1.title: PR correlation
pillar1.body: Tie cleaning cadence to performance ratio recovery trends, not just calendar washing.

pillar2.title: Predictive scheduling (ORION)
pillar2.body: Dust-storm triggers and yield shortfall alerts, moving from reactive to predictive O&M on Taypro fleets.

prose.heading: Why utility plants need cleaning-aware data intelligence
prose.p1: Most plant monitoring stacks were built for inverter availability, not for soiling loss quantification or cleaning coverage audit. When PR drifts, operators debate whether the issue is soiling, curtailment, or equipment, without block-level cleaning evidence.
prose.p2: NECTYR closes the execution gap: route completion, exceptions, robot health, and monthly panel-volume reconciliation for OPEX billing. Asset managers correlate cleaning events with PR recovery instead of dispatching blind crew visits.
prose.p3: ORION extends this into plant intelligence: weather feeds, soiling models, and yield analytics designed for Taypro fleet customers. ORION is on the roadmap with early access for select asset owners, not a separate product for non-Taypro fleets today.
prose.p4: 11 billion+ annual panel passes create a labelled dataset competitors cannot replicate without equivalent field presence, but awards and throughput claims require your own pilot PR validation.

steps.heading: How plant data intelligence rolls out on Taypro fleets
steps.subheading: From robot deployment to ORION early access.
steps.step0Body: Cleaning execution generates block-level telemetry, foundation for all analytics.
steps.step1Body: Correlate cleaning cycles with SCADA PR trends per block or feeder.
steps.step2Body: NECTYR reschedules around rain and dust storms, reducing wasted passes.
steps.step3Body: Monthly reconciliation and quarterly cycle adjustments for multi-site IPPs.
steps.step4Body: Plant intelligence pilots for qualifying Taypro AMC customers, NDA required.

highlight.heading: The data question
highlight.lead: Can you prove which blocks were cleaned before PR recovered?
highlight.body: Generic dashboards show energy after the fact. Taypro ties labelled cleaning events to array geography, the prerequisite for credible plant data intelligence.

proseSecondary.heading: Live today vs roadmap
proseSecondary.p1: NECTYR is production software on deployed fleets, scheduling, tickets, coverage exports, OPEX settlement. Use it today on Taypro CAPEX and OPEX programmes.
proseSecondary.p2: ORION capabilities described on taypro.in, predictive scheduling, enhanced yield analytics, optional sensing, are under active development. Early access is offered to select utility IPPs under NDA.
proseSecondary.p3: Do not substitute this page for a product datasheet. Request an ORION briefing for current pilot scope and integration requirements.

faq.heading: Solar plant data intelligence, FAQ
faq.subheading: NECTYR live capabilities and ORION roadmap.
faq.q0: What is the difference between NECTYR and ORION?
faq.a0: NECTYR is live fleet operations software. ORION is the plant intelligence layer on the roadmap, PR analytics, predictive scheduling, yield forecasting.
faq.a1: No. ORION complements SCADA and CMMS. Electrical monitoring stays in your existing stack.
faq.a2: ORION is in active development with early access for select Taypro fleet customers. NECTYR is live on 150+ sites.
faq.q3: Can I buy ORION without Taypro robots?
faq.a3: ORION is designed for Taypro cleaning telemetry. Enterprise discussions may apply for mixed fleets, contact us.
faq.a4: Cleaning routes, coverage, robot health, faults, weather reschedules, and monthly panel counts for OPEX billing.
faq.q5: How does this relate to fleet monitoring software?
faq.a5: Fleet monitoring page focuses on NECTYR ops keywords. This page focuses on plant intelligence and yield analytics positioning.
faq.q6: What is the 11 billion passes figure?
faq.a6: Cumulative annual cleaning pass volume across deployed fleets, labelled events feeding analytics roadmap.
faq.a7: Early access under NDA for qualifying AMC customers, typically at renewal on existing Taypro fleets.
faq.q8: Does data intelligence help SECI compliance?
faq.a8: Documented cleaning coverage supports technical due diligence, align with your tender's evidence requirements.
faq.q9: Is there an API for portfolio systems?
faq.a9: Enterprise integration discussions available, scope depends on deployment model.
faq.a10: NECTYR cites 95% field-verified reschedule accuracy, see performance methodology for claim definitions.
faq.a11: Deploy robots with NECTYR, establish PR baselines, then discuss ORION at AMC renewal.
